text:
Once upon a time in an old forest stood a cozy little cottage. Inside this cottage lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He was known far and wide for his endless wisdom. Every day, he'd watch the sun dip lower in the sky.

One evening, as the orange hues painted the sky in shades of pink and purple, Oliver heard a peculiar knock on his door.

Opening it quietly, he found a young squirrel with a bright new coat. The squirrel looked tired.

"Where are you from?" Oliver asked.

"I've come seeking wisdom," the squirrel began. "Every day I find myself yearning for something I can't name. I have adventures... yet I miss something important."

"What is it you miss?" Oliver asked.

"I miss... my shadow."

That's unusual. What do shadows do?"

"Nothing... I don't have a shadow."

Well, that seems sad.

Oliver chuckled. "Tell me more."
